methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
OpenStar, a Seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Ratu Mataira and Thomas Berry, has raised $35M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private peers and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector, including later-stage entrants such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression approach, which maps typical step-up multiples from Seed to next-stage funding, implies a $25.0B valuation using a 2.5x multiplier applied against a $10.0B total-raised proxy. The Peer-Median method triangulates across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A)—yielding a $150M implied value, with a range spanning $110M to $200M.

The two methods produce a wide triangulated range: $128M (low), $12.6B (median), and $28.7B (high). The significant divergence between peer-median and stage-progression results reflects inherent uncertainty in applying Seed-stage multiples and the sparse precedent for stage transitions in frontier climate technology. Key uncertainties include the appropriate comparable set adjustment for frontier-tech risk, the reliability of total-raised-based proxies for early-stage valuation, and the extent to which later-stage peer valuations reflect OpenStar's current position. The peer-median estimate of $150M likely represents a floor given Seed positioning.
